title: buildah --userns-uid-map and --userns-gid-map is not validated correctly
body:
This was first reported by @Luap99 in https://github.com/containers/storage/issues/739.  This issue has two issues.  One is in the storage side of things that's being fixed with https://github.com/containers/storage/pull/740.  The second is Buildah is not sending the value of --userns-uid-map-user and --userns-gid-map-group to NewIDMappings() as it should to validate the value passed to the option. I'll make another issue in Buildah.

Original Issue note:
<!--
If you are reporting a new issue, make sure that we do not have any duplicates
already open. You can ensure this by searching the issue list for this
repository. If there is a duplicate, please close your issue and add a comment
to the existing issue instead.

If you suspect your issue is a bug, please edit your issue description to
include the BUG REPORT INFORMATION shown below. If you fail to provide this
information within 7 days, we cannot debug your issue and will close it. We
will, however, reopen it if you later provide the information.

---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST
-->

**Description**

The `--userns-uid-map` and `--userns-gid-map` flag validation does not work correctly. Something like `--userns-uid-map bogus` is silently ignored. However `--userns-uid-map 123` fails with an error. So it looks like the validation only works if the argument contains a number.

This works (mappings are ignored):
`buildah bud --userns-gid-map a --userns-uid-map b .`
This fails:
`buildah bud --userns-gid-map a --userns-uid-map b1 .`

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
1. `buildah bud --userns-gid-map a --userns-uid-map b .`

**Describe the results you received:**
Image builds with default mappings.

**Describe the results you expected:**
`error initializing ID mappings: userns-uid-map setting is malformed`

**Output of `buildah version`:**

```
Version:         1.17.0-dev
Go Version:      go1.14.9
Image Spec:      1.0.1-dev
Runtime Spec:    1.0.2-dev
CNI Spec:        0.4.0
libcni Version:  v0.7.2-0.20190904153231-83439463f784
image Version:   5.6.0
Git Commit:      678da1d8
Built:           Tue Sep 22 19:48:26 2020
OS/Arch:         linux/amd64
```

comments.body: Closing this out.  I was confused between `--userns-gid-map` and `--userns-gid-map-group`, ditto the equivalent user.  When using the `--user-gid-map-group` option, we are validating the parameter with NewIDMappings as we should be.
Reopening, these fields do have a formatting problem, working up a fix now.

Closed via #2748
I'm not sure why GitHub didn't do so automatically.
